body_txt: Teaming up The Smart Guy with The Big Guy (or the Evil Genius with The Brute ). Sometimes, Brains and Brawn serve as heroes, but other times they're villains (forming an Evil Duo , often the Quirky Miniboss Squad or part of a Terrible Trio , sometimes being Bantering Baddie Buddies , and very frequently a Bumbling Henchmen Duo ). They may even be Those Two Guys . Either way, there tends to be some element of comedy inherent in their natures: the Brain may be condescending or even mean to the Brawn, who just shrugs it off ( or doesn't even realize he's been insulted ). In a pair like this, The Smart Guy gathers information or comes up with The Plan that will let The Big Guy make the best use of his strength. Other times, the Smart Guy provides items and equipment for the Big Guy to use. In return, the Big Guy must use his might to protect the Smart Guy, since the Smart Guy is a lot more vulnerable and less equipped for direct combat. Typically, since one person has brains but not brawn and the other brawn but not brains , the brawn half of this trope is also an example of Dumb Muscle . When you have one character with both Brains and Brawn, you've got yourself a Genius Bruiser or a Badass Bookworm , depending on which element is dominant. Sometimes both characters form this dynamic; both capable in combat but one more 'brainy' and the other more 'brawny.' If combined with Big Guy, Little Guy , the Little Guy is almost always the Brains and the Big Guy the Brawn. See also, Beauty, Brains, and Brawn , a feminine variation of the Trope with a third member — a Beauty — added, Strong Girl, Smart Guy , for when the Brawn is a girl and the Brains is a guy, and Smart Jerk and Nice Moron where the Brains is the Smart Jerk while the Brawn is the Nice Moron. A Technician/Performer Team-Up often has the "technician" as the brains and the "performer" as the brawn. A Sword and Sorcerer pairing can also fit this trope when the magic half of the pair is a Squishy Wizard or the White Mage . A Sub-Trope of Brains Versus Brawn . When adding examples, only list pairings where the Brawn is physically stronger than the Brain. If he's not superior when it comes to physical effort, then that pairing is does not fit this trope.
